stream_samples.text: claude prompt cache hit-rate dropped to ~40% on our system prompts since 04/22. used to be 90%+. token bill nearly 2x.
Extended thinking output truncating at ~32k tokens mid-reasoning, response just... stops. seen on sonnet 4.6 and 4.7.
[#9420 P1] Batch API for long-context jobs sitting in queue 18+ hours past SLA, customer eval pipeline blocked
claude code agent loop edge case — when a tool returns an error, the model retries the same tool 8 times before giving up. burns context fast.
MCP server connection drops every ~45min on long sessions. reconnect handshake works but in-flight tool calls are lost.
Computer-use vision recall on dense UIs (think Linear, Notion sidebars) is noticeably worse than the demo videos. clicking wrong elements ~15% of the time.
cache_read_input_tokens dropped from 88% to 41% across the top-50 platform customers since the inference fleet rotation 04/22
thinking budget honored intermittently — set 32k, getting ~12k actual on Sonnet 4.7 about 1 in 4 times
[alert] cache.read_hit_rate < 50% threshold breached for 14 of 16 windows, region: us-east, model: claude-sonnet-4.7
Files API: PDF >50MB returns 200 but file_id refers to truncated content, only first 12 pages retained on retrieval
Streaming response on /v1/messages occasionally drops the final stop_reason event, our SDK never resolves the promise
claude code's planning mode genuinely changed how I refactor. shipping 2x more in a day. quietly excellent.
is the 1M context window beta widely available now or still gated? docs are unclear
platform-eng triage: 9 of 12 P1s this week are cache-related, all hitting our top-revenue accounts

top_epic.title: Restore prompt cache hit-rate above 85% on platform system prompts
top_epic.rationale: 2,480 signals across X, Slack Connect, Datadog and Zendesk converge on the same regression: prompt cache hit-rate dropped from ~88% to 41% across the top-50 platform customers after the 04/22 inference fleet rotation. Token bills are nearly doubling on accounts running stable system prompts — and 9 of 12 P1s this week are cache-related, all hitting top-revenue accounts. Extended thinking truncation is a secondary symptom of the same fleet change.
top_epic.verdict: Revenue-critical — cache hit-rate is the single biggest knob on platform-customer cost-of-goods.
top_epic.recommendation: Roll back the affinity policy change, ship a per-customer cache hit-rate SLO, and gate inference fleet rotations on a cache-replay benchmark.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt cache hit-rate > 85% on top-50 platform customers for 7 consecutive days
cache_read_input_tokens ratio restored to pre-04/22 baseline (within 3 percentage points)
Per-customer cache hit-rate SLO published with paging on sustained < 70%
Inference fleet rotation CI gate that replays last 24h of cached system prompts pre-rollout
